security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged potential issues (external_commands, weak_crypto) but all are false positives. The file is a markdown documentation skill using markdown code block delimiters (backticks) which were misidentified as shell execution. No executable code, network calls, or security risks present. Safe for publication.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
high_findings.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged YAML syntax and keywords as weak crypto. The '|' character is YAML multiline syntax, 'backstory' is a CrewAI field name, and 'encryption' keywords are absent. No cryptographic code present.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain YAML configuration syntax (multiline pipes), CrewAI field names (backstory), and general text. No encryption algorithms or crypto operations exist in this documentation file.

medium_findings.title: External Commands - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown code block delimiters (backticks) as shell execution. The file SKILL.md is a documentation file using markdown code fences (```python), not actual shell commands. No command execution risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown code fences use backticks for syntax highlighting. The backtick pattern appears at the start of code blocks, not in executable contexts.

content.user_title: Build Multi-Agent AI Systems with CrewAI
content.value_statement: Creating collaborative AI agent teams requires understanding CrewAI framework patterns. This skill provides expert guidance on agent design, task definition, and crew orchestration for building production-ready multi-agent systems.
actual_capabilities: Design agent roles with specific goals and backstories
Define tasks with expected outputs and dependencies
Set up memory and tool integration for agents
Implement planning features for complex workflows
limitations: Best suited for structured, predictable workflows
Can be verbose for simple automation tasks
Flows feature is relatively new and evolving

use_cases.title: Research and Content Creation Pipeline
use_cases.description: Create a crew with researcher, writer, and editor agents to automate research and content production workflows

use_cases.title: Data Analysis Team
use_cases.description: Build specialized analyst agents for data collection, processing, and visualization tasks
use_cases.target_user: Data scientists, analysts, business intelligence teams

use_cases.title: Code Review and Documentation
use_cases.description: Automate code review, documentation generation, and quality assurance with specialized agent crews
use_cases.target_user: Software developers, DevOps engineers, technical writers

prompt_templates.title: Basic Crew Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple CrewAI crew with a researcher agent and a writer agent. The researcher should find information on {topic} and the writer should create a blog post from the research.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with CrewAI for simple two-agent workflows

prompt_templates.title: Hierarchical Process
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a hierarchical CrewAI process with a manager agent that coordinates three worker agents: a data collector, an analyst, and a reporter. Use gpt-4o as the manager LLM.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building coordinated multi-agent systems with oversight

prompt_templates.title: Task Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a crew where task B depends on the output of task A. Task A should gather data and task B should analyze that data and produce a summary report.

prompt_templates.title: Planning-Enabled Crew
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure a CrewAI crew with planning enabled. Create three agents: planner, executor, and validator. Enable planning so the crew generates an execution plan before running tasks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Using CrewAI planning feature for complex workflows

output_examples.input: Create a research crew with two agents
output_examples.output: A basic crew configuration with researcher and writer agents, including YAML config files and Python decorator-based setup, demonstrating agent roles, goals, backstories, and task definitions.

output_examples.input: Show me how to use hierarchical process
output_examples.prompt: Explain and show code for setting up a manager-led hierarchical process where agents work under manager coordination
output_examples.output: Complete example with manager_llm configuration, worker agent definitions, and how the manager delegates tasks to specialized agents.

best_practices: Define specific, focused agent roles rather than generic ones - use titles like 'Senior Research Analyst' instead of 'Researcher'
Always specify expected_output for tasks to ensure consistent, usable results
Start with 3-5 agents and add more only when coordination overhead is justified
anti_patterns: Creating vague agent roles without clear expertise areas leads to poor task delegation
Skipping expected_output definition causes inconsistent results and hard-to-chain tasks
Using too many agents creates coordination overhead and slower execution without proportional benefit

faq.question: What is CrewAI?
faq.answer: CrewAI is a Python framework for building multi-agent AI systems where agents have defined roles, goals, and backstories, and can collaborate on complex tasks through orchestrated workflows.

faq.question: Do I need Python experience?
faq.answer: Yes, CrewAI is a Python-only framework. You should be comfortable with Python syntax, YAML configuration, and basic OOP concepts to use it effectively.

faq.question: What are the main process types?
faq.answer: CrewAI supports sequential (tasks run in order) and hierarchical (manager coordinates workers) processes. Sequential is simpler; hierarchical is better for complex coordination.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude or GPT models?
faq.answer: Yes, CrewAI works with any LLM that has an OpenAI-compatible API, including GPT-4, Claude, and open-source models through various providers.

faq.question: How is this different from LangChain?
faq.answer: CrewAI focuses specifically on multi-agent orchestration with role-based agents, while LangChain provides broader LLM tooling. CrewAI abstracts the agent coordination pattern.

faq.question: What are the limitations?
faq.answer: CrewAI is Python-only, works best with structured workflows, can be verbose for simple tasks, and the Flows feature is still maturing.
